questionText: A curve is given by $x = t + 4$ and $y = 2t$. Find the coordinates of the points on the curve when $t = -1$ and when $t = 3$.

steps.explanation: Each value of $t$ gives one point. We substitute the two values of $t$ in turn, keeping the same $t$ in both equations each time.
Putting $t = -1$ into the $x$-equation gives the horizontal position of the first point.
Using the same $t = -1$ in the $y$-equation gives the matching vertical position.
Combining the two results gives the first point on the curve.
Now repeat with $t = 3$; this gives the horizontal position of the second point.
The same $t = 3$ in the $y$-equation gives its vertical position.
Pairing the values gives the second point, so the curve passes through both $(3,\,-2)$ and $(7,\,6)$.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The curve passes through $(3,\, -2)$ when $t = -1$ and $(7,\, 6)$ when $t = 3$.
